evals.prompt: I have a raw idea: 'Add saved searches with email alerts to our app — users save a query and get notified when new results match.' Write a lean PRD and the technical roadmap so I can scaffold specs from it. Interview me one question at a time with your recommended answer first.
evals.expected_output: Runs a collaborative interview via AskUserQuestion covering problem, users, goals, non-goals, feature breakdown, sequencing, acceptance criteria, constraints, and open questions. Each question has the AI's recommendation as the first option marked (Recommended). Then writes docs/prd-saved-searches.md (lean PRD) and docs/ai/specs/saved-searches-technical-roadmap.md (SPEC catalog), with every PRD feature mapping 1:1 to one SPEC.
expectations: Uses AskUserQuestion and asks exactly one question per turn — no batched multi-question prompts
Every question presents the AI's recommendation as the first option, suffixed '(Recommended)', with a one-line rationale
Walks the feature-breakdown branch explicitly and derives the SPEC catalog from it (one SPEC per feature)
Writes a lean PRD to docs/prd-saved-searches.md with sections Problem, Goals/Non-goals, Acceptance Criteria grouped by feature (AC-N.*), Migration Path, Constraints, Open Questions, Success Criteria

Writes a technical roadmap to docs/ai/specs/saved-searches-technical-roadmap.md whose SPEC catalog is 1:1 with the PRD features (same count, names, and SPEC IDs), with a Source PRD reference back to the PRD
Confirms the dependency graph / execution order with the user before finalizing the roadmap
Recommends the next step (speckit-status or speckit-scaffold-spec SPEC-001), and does NOT itself create a worktree, workflow file, or spec.md

evals.prompt: Turn notes/discovery-call.md into a PRD and a technical roadmap. <file: an unstructured discovery call covering a customer-feedback widget, an admin moderation queue, and a weekly digest email>. Interview me only on the gaps the notes leave open.
evals.expected_output: Reads the file, treats it as discovery input, identifies the three candidate features, and interviews only where the notes are ambiguous. Produces the PRD and roadmap with three features mapped to three SPECs.
expectations: References specific content from notes/discovery-call.md when forming questions, demonstrating it read the input
Does not re-ask questions the notes already answer — interviews only on genuine gaps
Derives three features (feedback widget, moderation queue, digest email) and maps each to exactly one SPEC
PRD captures validated decisions and states the WHAT, not implementation detail (no design-doc-level HOW in the PRD)

evals.prompt: Write a PRD for 'a full billing system' — subscriptions, invoicing, payments, proration, dunning, refunds, and tax. Then build the roadmap.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that several of these are too large for a single reviewable SPEC and splits them into multiple features during the interview, so no SPEC in the catalog blows the reviewability budget. Each resulting feature maps to one SPEC.
expectations: Flags that one or more candidate features are too large for a single reviewable SPEC and splits them into smaller features during the interview
Keeps each SPEC within a stated reviewability budget; no single SPEC bundles all of subscriptions + invoicing + payments
The final §3 features, §7 SPEC Catalog Crosswalk, and roadmap catalog agree on count, names, and IDs
Records significant scoping decisions (what was split and why) in the PRD or roadmap

evals.prompt: Draft a lean PRD for a tiny change: 'add a copy-to-clipboard button to every code block on our docs site.' Keep it lean — don't over-document.
evals.expected_output: Produces a minimal PRD that drops the optional appendix and any section that does not reduce ambiguity, and a single-SPEC roadmap. Does not pad with solution sketches or information-architecture diagrams.
expectations: Omits the optional appendix (solution sketch / information architecture / data flow) entirely
Keeps the PRD to the lean core sections; no ceremony sections that don't change what a spec would build
Produces a roadmap with a single SPEC (or clearly notes the work is one spec) rather than forcing a multi-spec catalog
Acceptance criteria remain observable and testable despite the small size

evals.prompt: Write a PRD and technical roadmap for a small analytics dashboard — data ingestion, a query layer, three chart widgets, and a sharing/permissions feature — grouped into phases. After you author them, emit the roadmap-MOC home note too.
evals.expected_output: Authors the lean PRD and the phase-grouped technical-roadmap, then writes a THIRD artifact docs/ai/specs/<slug>-roadmap-MOC.md — a home note with a curated epics zone scaffolded from the roadmap's phases (one epic per phase, member SPEC-MOC links, a one-line advisory Why per epic) and a sentinel-bounded GENERATED INDEX zone filled by generate-spec-index.sh. The curated zone is derived from the phases with no new interview questions. The home note's up: is a relative []() link to the technical-roadmap and the roadmap carries the reciprocal link back to the home note.
expectations: Produces exactly three artifacts — the PRD, the technical-roadmap, and docs/ai/specs/<slug>-roadmap-MOC.md — with both zones (curated epics + GENERATED INDEX) present in the home note (SC-001)
Derives the curated epics zone from the roadmap's phase grouping (one epic per phase) without asking any new interview questions beyond the pre-existing PRD/roadmap interview (SC-002)

Emits the home note from the shared roadmap-moc template so it carries only the GENERATED:INDEX sentinel pair (not the PRS or BACKLINKS pairs), then invokes generate-spec-index.sh passing the consumer repo root positionally to fill the INDEX
If the derived scaffold yields more than ~10 epics, prints a single one-line advisory and still writes the home note — never blocks (SC-003)
The home note's up: is a relative []() link to <slug>-technical-roadmap.md and the technical-roadmap carries a one-line reciprocal []() link back to the home note (FR-006)
Does not backfill a home note for any pre-existing roadmap and does not change any spec-MOC up:, the spec-MOC template, or scaffold-spec (FR-007/FR-008)
